# posthog-flags-to-notion

Sync PostHog feature flags to a Notion database. Resolves opaque group IDs to real customer names so your whole team can see which flags are on and for whom.

## Usage

There are three ways to use this tool:

1. **Claude Code agent** — copy `agents/feature-flags.md` to `~/.claude/agents/`. Requires the Notion MCP server. No npm install needed.
2. **MCP server** — `npx posthog-flags-to-notion --mcp` exposes tools (`list_flags`, `sync_flags_to_notion`, `lookup_group`, `flags_for_group`). Works with Cursor and other MCP clients. Requires `POSTHOG_API_KEY`, `POSTHOG_PROJECT_ID`, `NOTION_API_KEY`, `NOTION_DATABASE_ID`.
3. **CLI** — `npx posthog-flags-to-notion` runs a one-shot sync. Same env vars as the MCP server. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

## How it works

1. Fetches all feature flags from the PostHog API
2. Parses targeting rules — flags targeting specific groups get their IDs extracted; percentage rollouts get a plain-English summary
3. Resolves group IDs to human-readable names using PostHog's Groups API
4. Writes results to a Notion database — creates rows for new flags, updates existing ones

## Configuration

Required env vars (for CLI and MCP server):
- `POSTHOG_API_KEY` — PostHog personal API key
- `POSTHOG_PROJECT_ID` — your PostHog project ID
- `NOTION_API_KEY` — Notion integration token
- `NOTION_DATABASE_ID` — Notion database to write flags to

Optional:
- `POSTHOG_HOST` — defaults to `https://us.posthog.com`, use `https://eu.posthog.com` for EU
- `POSTHOG_GROUP_TYPE_INDEX` — which group type to resolve (default: `0`)
- `POSTHOG_GROUP_PROPERTY_KEY` — the property key in flag filters (default: `project_id`)
- `NOTION_DIRECTORY_DATABASE_ID` — optional second database for group name/ID lookup
- `SKIP_SURVEY_FLAGS` — skip survey targeting flags (default: `true`)

## Notion database schema

The tool auto-creates these columns:
- **Description** (title): flag name from PostHog
- **Flag Key** (url): link to the flag in PostHog
- **Status** (select): Active or Inactive
- **Targeting** (text): summary of targeting rules
- **Groups Enabled** (text): comma-separated group names
- **Last Updated** (date): date of last sync
- **Notes** (text): manual column, never overwritten
